behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: This checks cross-module handoff/orchestration (dispatcher -> per-reviewer invocation payloads). Unit tests of isolated builders can miss wiring mistakes (wrong role mapped to wrong instruction pack). Rejected Agentic because the behavior text is about inputs being sent correctly, not model judgment quality: "sends ... correct reviewer-specific instruction pack".
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with a fixture contract and stub reviewer clients; assert each reviewer receives the identical source document plus the correct role-specific instruction pack and no cross-role mixups.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output quality (extracting legal terms from natural language). A stubbed extractor would hide failures in actual extraction behavior. Rejected Workflow because this is a single reviewer’s model-dependent step, not a multi-agent sequence requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real clause extractor on representative contracts and assert required key-term categories are captured with acceptable coverage/accuracy thresholds.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation wiring/config propagation across components, where unit-only tests can miss serialization/mapping mistakes at module boundaries. Rejected Agentic because behavior text limits scope to parameter passing: "configuration is passed correctly ... invocation" (not whether model flags correctly).
behaviors.test_description: Execute risk-assessor call path with a concrete policy config and stubbed assessor endpoint; assert the outbound invocation contains the exact policy fields/values and expected schema.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Detection of indirect/euphemistic risky language is model-judgment quality, which fakes cannot validate. Real LLM behavior is required to avoid false confidence. Rejected Workflow because only one model stage is being validated here.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live risk assessor on contracts containing explicit and euphemistic policy violations; assert it flags the targeted passages at expected severity under the configured policy.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is inter-module data handoff (context propagation into compliance invocation). Unit-only checks can miss real wiring mismatches. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is strictly about passing inputs correctly: "are passed correctly ... invocation."
behaviors.test_description: Drive the compliance-check invocation path with explicit jurisdiction/regulatory profile fixtures and a stub checker; assert the outbound request carries exact values and structure.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real AI compliance judgment against legal/regulatory text. Mocked outputs would mask true decision quality problems. Rejected Workflow because this behavior evaluates a single reviewer’s model decision, not cross-step agentic consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the live compliance checker on labeled contract samples (compliant/non-compliant by profile) and assert determination quality against expected outcomes.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Risk is in module interaction/data-shape handoff into merge logic (multi-source conflict handling), which unit tests of isolated helper functions may miss. Rejected Agentic because behavior text targets deterministic merge semantics: "merge step preserves both assessments with attribution," not correctness of model judgments.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merge pipeline with realistic reviewer outputs containing same-clause conflicts; assert final unified report retains both assessments and reviewer attribution without overwriting.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step agent pipeline with multiple model-dependent reviewer stages plus aggregation. Pass/fail depends on sequencing and state handoff across several agentic outputs, so Workflow is required. Rejected Agentic because single-step model validation would not prove cross-step completeness ("dispatch all three ... collect outputs ... merge ... without losing any findings").
behaviors.test_description: Run the full pipeline on real contracts with live reviewers; verify all three reviewer outputs are produced, carried through collection, and present in the merged report with no dropped findings.
